steps.title: Identify the behavioral shift electric lighting would cause
steps.content:
The researchers conclude that electric lighting in the late nineteenth century prompted a behavioral shift visible in how fiction references time.

The key prediction: if lighting enabled evening activity, novels published after its spread should contain significantly more references to late-night activities than earlier novels.

steps.title: Find the evidence matching this prediction
steps.content:
Answer D directly demonstrates the predicted shift: novels after 1880 contain significantly more references to activities after 10 p.m., confirming that electric lighting enabled the behavioral change toward evening activity.
  • Choice A — 10 a.m. references before/after 1800: Goes the wrong direction — a daytime decline does not demonstrate the predicted shift toward evening activity, and the timeframe (1800) predates the researchers' late-nineteenth-century mechanism. ✗
  • Choice B — implied references becoming more common than clock phrases: Addresses reference format and style rather than the substantive shift toward late-night activity; says nothing about when in the day activities occur. ✗
  • Choice C — noon vs. midnight used with similar frequency: Shows no shift over time and does not isolate the post-lighting period; equal frequency contradicts a behavioral shift toward evening. ✗
  • Choice D — post-1880 novels contain significantly more references to activities after 10 p.m.: Directly matches the predicted behavioral shift — more late-night activity references appear after the spread of electric lighting. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
